metadata.title: Build a Self-as-Project Brand Runner with Claude
metadata.topic: BRANDING AND AI · SELF AS PROJECT
metadata.purpose: Show students in Nina Harris' Branding and AI course how to run their own career through the Self-as-Project pipeline with Claude drafting and the human signing off at every gate, with an accountability log recording each decision.
metadata.audience: Students and practitioners in a university branding course with an AI component
sources: Branding and AI, Chapter 3 + Introduction (Nina Harris)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'ClaudeComposerAsk916' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
END: palette=claude but the outro is 'un-annotated' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

metadata.reformat: center-cut, focus-aware; pantry/<bid>-916.* is the human replacement slot; -916 overrides honored

beats.narration_text: Talofa — this is Liam, in for Bear. For Nina Harris' course, Branding and AI, here is the question we are running today: run your own career through the same pipeline the book runs a brand through — archetype, position, voice, portfolio thesis — with Claude drafting each stage and you signing it off. The accountability log records which drafts you approved, edited, or rejected. The log proves which was which.

props.command: Self-as-Project brand build. Background: [BACKGROUND]. Target field: [FIELD]. Draft four artifacts, clearly labeled DRAFT — pending +1 sign-off: (1) Jungian archetype + shadow, (2) one-sentence position, (3) 3-sentence voice sample, (4) portfolio thesis. After each, state the specific human judgment required to approve it.
props.topic: BRANDING AND AI · SELF AS PROJECT
output: DRAFT position: engineer who ships AND frames
DRAFT voice: direct, concrete, no hedging
DRAFT thesis: every project proves judgment + execution + ship

beats.narration_text: Here is what breaks without the gated pipeline. You ask Claude to build your personal brand. It produces something that sounds like you — or like a plausible version of you based on your inputs. You paste it into your LinkedIn bio. You own a brand that the machine authored without a single committed decision from you. The AI-plus-one thesis: the agent drafts, the plus-one decides, and the log proves which was which.

props.sparkLine: The agent drafts. The plus-one decides.

beats.narration_text: The theory from Chapter Three and the Introduction. The Self-as-Project thread: run your own career through the same brand-building pipeline the course runs a product through. Four artifacts, in order: Jungian archetype with shadow, one-sentence position, three-sentence voice sample, portfolio thesis. At each stage, the model drafts and marks the output DRAFT — pending plus-one sign-off. The human reviews against specific evidence — your own behavior, your last five projects, your actual audience — and either approves, edits, or rejects. The accountability log records every decision so the pipeline's output is yours, not the model's.

The centerpiece: four-artifact pipeline as a vertical flow. Left column: AI drafts each artifact — DRAFT archetype, DRAFT position, DRAFT voice, DRAFT portfolio thesis. Each carries the label DRAFT — pending plus-one sign-off. Right column: the plus-one gate for each artifact — the specific human judgment required before the artifact can be approved. Archetype gate: does this ring true to people who know you? Position gate: can you name two people you have actually positioned against? Voice gate: does your last five pieces of writing match this? Portfolio thesis gate: review your last three projects — do they prove this? The accountability log at the bottom records which drafts were approved, edited, or rejected. Source: Branding and AI, Nina Harris, Chapter Three and Introduction.

Honesty about what the pipeline does not do. The tool does not auto-finalize any artifact — the whole point is that the sign-off is human. And the pipeline produces brand artifacts, not job applications or resume writing. The accountability question is whether you can name and defend every brand decision — the log exists to make that possible.

artifactLines: The tool does not auto-finalize any artifact — the sign-off is always human.
The pipeline produces brand artifacts, not resumes or job applications.
The accountability log proves which decisions were yours — not which the machine made.
The model refuses to mark anything final without a recorded human approval.

beats.narration_text: Let us recap with Claude. Self-as-Project Brand Runner. Four lines.

artifactLines: The AI+1 thesis made operational: agent drafts, you decide, the log proves which was which.
Four artifacts in order: archetype, position, voice sample, portfolio thesis.
Every artifact is labeled DRAFT with a specific human judgment required before approval.
The accountability log is what makes the pipeline yours rather than the machine's.

beats.narration_text: That's the short version. The full video also covers self-as-project brand runner — watch Build a Self-as-Project Brand Runner with Claude for the whole story. The link is right below.
